page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance allocates 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options for persistent disk requirements.

The hardware profile offers a balanced resource allocation suitable for entry-level workloads. Because the vCPU allocation is dedicated, the instance avoids the performance variability associated with shared-core or burstable configurations. This makes it a predictable option for workloads requiring steady-state execution without the overhead of larger instance classes.

Appropriate workloads for the n4a-standard-2 include lightweight web serving, small-scale databases, development environments, and basic background processing tasks. The absence of local storage and hardware acceleration limits its use for high-performance storage-heavy or machine learning tasks, positioning it primarily as a cost-efficient, general-purpose utility node.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory. Providing a balanced 1:4 ratio of vCPUs to memory with 4.0 GB per core, this instance delivers predictable performance without the resource contention of shared-core alternatives. It features no local storage or integrated GPUs, making it dependent on network-attached storage. The instance is designed for entry-level, steady-state workloads such as lightweight web serving, application development, and small-scale databases. Its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution, making it a reliable and cost-efficient choice for general utility workloads that do not require high-performance storage or hardware acceleration.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine with dedicated compute resources and a standard memory-to-core ratio for predictable general-purpose workloads.

No local storage or bundled GPU hardware
Predictable performance without shared-core resource contention
